articles.title: Google moves Delhi HC against trademark infringement ruling in Hindware AdWords case
articles.summary: A Division Bench comprising Justices V. Kameswar Rao and Manmeet P.S. Arora on Friday issued notice to Hindware on Google's appeal and listed the matter for further hearing on July 24.

articles.title: Why are airplane windows round? The deadly lesson behind their shape
articles.summary: Airplane windows are usually oval or rounded for a structural reason, not simply looks. Openings in a pressurised fuselage must manage stress, and abrupt corners create higher local stress concentrations than curved edges.

articles.title: Reverse oil flow: India supplies gasoline to Russia - why it matters
articles.summary: Indian refiners are well equipped from a product-quality standpoint to supply the gasoline grades required by Russia. However, Oil minister Hardeep Singh Puri has said that Indian companies are not directly selling gasoline to Russia.

articles.title: Sourav Ganguly's Rs 2,500 crore steel plant: Here's where the Bengal project stands
articles.summary: Nearly 17 months after Sourav Ganguly said his Rs 2,500 crore steel plant would become operational within 18-20 months, the ambitious West Bengal project is yet to begin production.

articles.title: 'I've grown old': MS Dhoni tells fans, Captain Cool's candid gesture wins hearts
articles.summary: Dhoni pointed towards his grey beard and gestured that he had grown old. He then signalled that he could no longer run between the wickets the way he used to during his playing days

articles.title: SpaceX no longer a global leader: China's Long March 10B challenges Elon Musk's reusable rocket dominance
articles.summary: The achievement marks China's first successful recovery of an orbital-class reusable rocket booster and signals that Beijing is rapidly advancing technologies considered essential for the next era of affordable spaceflight.

articles.title: Govt says no to E10, cites ₹1 lakh crore per year investments in ethanol production financed by PSBs
articles.summary: The suggestion that every petrol pump should stock pure petrol, E10 and E20 simultaneously ignores the realities of India's fuel distribution network, says the petroleum ministry.

articles.title: Global wealth report: Why being a rich country doesn't always mean richer citizens
articles.summary: Average (mean) wealth is calculated by dividing a country's total household wealth by its adult population. Because it includes everyone, a relatively small number of billionaires and ultra-wealthy individuals can dramatically inflate the figure.

articles.title: Consumers shift to premium petrol amid E20 concerns; demand doubles at Delhi fuel stations: Sources
articles.summary: Delhi's fuel retail market is witnessing a sharp shift as more motorists opt for premium petrol variants such as XP95, XP100 and Speed 97 amid concerns surrounding E20 fuel, sources told Business Today TV.

articles.title: India, among world’s largest exporters of refined oil products, set to see 25% rise in petroleum product exports
articles.summary: Although India imports around 90% of its crude oil requirement, it has become one of the world's largest exporters of refined petroleum products by using its large and sophisticated refineries.

articles.title: ‘You know who we are :)’: This company’s unnamed 18th birthday ad is a masterclass on brand recall
articles.summary: The minimalist campaign was effectively a test of brand recall: could one colour, one tone of voice and one sentence be enough for readers to know exactly who was speaking?

articles.title: India should not blink first in US trade negotiations, says SBI report
articles.summary: India should avoid early trade concessions with the United States. Washington is using uncertainty as a bargaining instrument across many issues. India possesses unique strategic strengths that should be leveraged. The nation should play the long game and test US resolve.

articles.title: FIFA World Cup scores interest in India, but can ZEE5 win the subscription game?
articles.summary: With the tournament behind a paywall, Zee Entertainment appears to be betting on subscriptions even as advertisers remain cautious in a cricket-obsessed market.

articles.title: E20 costlier to produce than pure petrol: Government
articles.summary: The petroleum ministry clarified E20 petrol production costs versus pure petrol. Ethanol blending protects Indian consumers from volatile international oil prices. This program has saved India significant foreign exchange and crude oil imports.

articles.title: Ahead of UK trade deal roll out, India notifies lower customs duty for car imports
articles.summary: India-UK FTA to be effective from July 15, India has committed to lower import duty to 10% in 15 years for specified quota of cars.

articles.title: A weak monsoon could trigger a storm in India's economy
articles.summary: A weaker monsoon poses risks to India's rural economy and farm incomes. This could increase food inflation and slow down consumption demand in coming months. Agriculture and related sectors face the biggest impact from below-normal rainfall.

articles.title: Crude dips but eyes weekly gain as US-Iran conflict stokes supply fears
articles.summary: Oil prices saw a slight decrease but are set for significant weekly gains. Renewed military exchanges between the United States and Iran kept crude supply concerns elevated. The conflict has delayed the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, a critical energy chokepoint.

articles.title: India's GCCs redraw global leadership map
Nilekani’s Fundamentum launches Rs 2,200 crore Fund III
articles.summary: Fundamentum Partnership has launched its third fund with a Rs 2,200 crore target corpus. Nandan Nilekani anchors this fund, marking his largest venture capital commitment. The firm focuses on Series B investments in Indian technology companies ready for scaling.
